questions.text: A tangent to a circle intersects it in how many point(s)?
questions.explanation: By definition, a tangent is a line that touches the circle at exactly one point.

questions.text: A line intersecting a circle in two points is called a:
questions.explanation: A secant is a line that intersects a circle at two distinct points.

questions.text: A circle can have ______ parallel tangents at most.
questions.explanation: Parallel tangents can only exist at the two endpoints of a diameter.

questions.text: The common point of a tangent to a circle and the circle is called:
questions.explanation: The point where the tangent touches the circle is specifically known as the point of contact.

questions.text: The angle between a tangent and the radius through the point of contact is:
questions.explanation: Theorem 10.1 states that the tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius through the point of contact.

questions.text: How many tangents can be drawn to a circle from a point inside it?
questions.explanation: No tangent can be drawn to a circle from a point lying inside it because any line through it will intersect the circle at two points.

questions.text: The lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are:
questions.explanation: Theorem 10.2 states that tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal in length.

questions.text: A tangent PQ at a point P of a circle of radius 5 cm meets a line through the centre O at a point Q so that OQ = 12 cm. Length PQ is:
questions.explanation: Using Pythagoras: PQ² = OQ² - OP² = 12² - 5² = 144 - 25 = 119. PQ = √119.

questions.text: If TP and TQ are two tangents to a circle with centre O so that ∠POQ = 110°, then ∠PTQ is equal to:
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in quadrilateral OPTQ is 360°. Since ∠P = ∠Q = 90°, ∠PTQ = 180° - 110° = 70°.

questions.text: If tangents PA and PB from a point P to a circle with centre O are inclined to each other at angle of 80°, then ∠POA is equal to:
questions.explanation: ∠APB = 80°, so ∠AOB = 180 - 80 = 100°. Since OP bisects ∠AOB, ∠POA = 100/2 = 50°.

questions.text: The distance between two parallel tangents of a circle of radius 4 cm is:
questions.explanation: The distance between two parallel tangents is equal to the diameter of the circle (2 * 4 = 8 cm).

questions.text: In two concentric circles, the chord of the larger circle which touches the smaller circle is ______ at the point of contact.
questions.explanation: The radius of the smaller circle is perpendicular to the chord and thus bisects it.

questions.text: If a quadrilateral ABCD is drawn to circumscribe a circle, then AB + CD is equal to:
questions.explanation: For a circumscribed quadrilateral, the sums of opposite sides are equal.

questions.text: A parallelogram circumscribing a circle is a:
questions.explanation: A parallelogram whose sides touch a circle must have all sides equal, making it a rhombus.

questions.text: The angle subtended by the segment joining the points of contact of two parallel tangents at the centre is:
questions.explanation: Parallel tangents occur at endpoints of a diameter, which subtends 180° at the centre.

questions.text: If the angle between two tangents drawn from a point P to a circle of radius 'r' and centre O is 90°, then OP is:
questions.explanation: In this case, OAPB forms a square. The diagonal OP = √(r² + r²) = r√2.

questions.text: How many tangents can be drawn to a circle from a point on the circle?
questions.explanation: Exactly one tangent can be drawn to a circle at a point lying on the circle.

questions.text: The length of the tangent from an external point P at a distance of 13 cm from the centre of a circle of radius 5 cm is:
Two circles touch each other externally. The number of common tangents is:
questions.explanation: Two direct common tangents and one transverse common tangent at the point of contact.

questions.text: Two circles touch each other internally. The number of common tangents is:
questions.explanation: Only one common tangent can be drawn at the point where they touch internally.

questions.text: If the perimeter of a circumscribing quadrilateral is 30 cm, then the sum of a pair of opposite sides is:
questions.explanation: Since AB + CD = AD + BC, each sum is half of the perimeter (30/2 = 15).

questions.text: A line that touches the circle at only one point is a special case of a secant when:
options: The endpoints of its chord coincide
questions.explanation: A tangent is a limiting case of a secant when the two endpoints of its corresponding chord coincide.

questions.text: The angle between two tangents drawn from an external point to a circle is ______ to the angle subtended by the line segment joining the points of contact at the centre.
questions.explanation: The two angles add up to 180°, making them supplementary.

questions.text: From a point Q, the length of the tangent to a circle is 24 cm and the distance of Q from the centre is 25 cm. The radius of the circle is:
Tangents drawn at the ends of a diameter of a circle are:
questions.explanation: Both tangents are perpendicular to the same diameter, so they are parallel to each other.

questions.text: If two circles do not intersect or touch each other, the maximum number of common tangents is:
questions.explanation: There are 2 direct common tangents and 2 transverse common tangents.

questions.text: The distance between the points of contact of two parallel tangents is equal to the:
questions.explanation: Parallel tangents occur at the opposite ends of a diameter.

questions.text: Which of the following is true for a tangent?
options: It can pass through the centre
It is perpendicular to the radius at point of contact
questions.explanation: This is the fundamental property of a tangent in Theorem 10.1.

questions.text: If a chord of length 16 cm is at a distance of 6 cm from the centre of a circle, what is the radius?
questions.explanation: Using Pythagoras on half-chord: r² = 6² + 8² = 36 + 64 = 100. r = 10.

questions.text: The area of a circle whose diameter is equal to the distance between two parallel tangents (radius 5) is:
questions.explanation: Distance = diameter = 10 cm. Radius = 5 cm. Area = π(5)² = 25π.

questions.text: Two tangents TP and TQ are drawn from external point T. If ∠PTQ = 60°, then triangle TPQ is:
questions.explanation: Since TP=TQ, ∠TPQ = ∠TQP. In △TPQ, 60 + 2x = 180 => x = 60. All angles 60 makes it equilateral.

questions.text: The locus of the centre of a circle which touches a given line at a given point is a:
options: Line perpendicular to the given line
Line parallel to the given line
questions.explanation: The center must lie on the radius, which is perpendicular to the tangent at the point of contact.

questions.text: If radii of two concentric circles are 3 cm and 5 cm, the length of the chord of the larger circle which touches the smaller circle is:
questions.explanation: Half chord = √(5² - 3²) = 4 cm. Full chord = 2 * 4 = 8 cm.

questions.text: The number of tangents that can be drawn from a point 3 cm away from a circle of radius 5 cm is:
questions.explanation: The point is inside the circle (3 < 5), so no tangents can be drawn.

questions.text: If the angle between two radii is 130°, then the angle between the tangents at their ends is:
questions.explanation: Tangents and radii form a quadrilateral where opposite angles are supplementary (180 - 130 = 50).

questions.text: Point P is 26 cm away from the centre of a circle and the length of tangent drawn from P to the circle is 24 cm. Radius is:
What is the relation between the lengths of two tangents drawn from an external point?
questions.explanation: According to Theorem 10.2, they are always equal.

questions.text: In a circle of radius 7 cm, the tangent at a point A is 24 cm long. The distance of the end of the tangent from the centre is:
The sum of opposite sides of a quadrilateral circumscribing a circle are:
questions.explanation: For any quadrilateral circumscribing a circle, the sum of opposite sides is equal (AB + CD = BC + DA).

questions.text: If a point P is on the circle, how many tangents can be drawn from P?
questions.explanation: There is only one tangent at any point on the circumference of a circle.
